question: Will Kite Robotics and Halvorsen Motors sign a component-supply partnership before the fall production freeze?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

production_freeze_prevents_new_supply_co.rule: if production_freeze.status == 'active' then no new component_supply_partnership can be signed

time_constraint.rule: partnership_signed must occur before production_freeze_activated for the partnership to be valid before freeze

committee_decision_requires_minimum_vote.rule: committee_decision_record must have vote_count_approve + vote_count_reject >= 2 for validity

offer_exclusivity_rule.rule: kite_robotics cannot have both volume_discount_offer and co_marketing_clause_offer active simultaneously
offer_exclusivity_rule.evidence: Kite can offer a 12% volume discount or a co-marketing clause, not both

amara_diallo.why_consequential: initiates partnership proposal and decides which offer (volume discount or co-marketing) to include
affordances: sign contract on behalf of kite_robotics

erik_halvorsen.why_consequential: delegated supplier onboarding to procurement committee; can override committee decision or expedite process

sofia_brandt.why_consequential: chairs monthly meetings and controls agenda; decides when proposal is reviewed

unresolved_mechanisms: exact date of next procurement committee meeting is unknown; may not occur before freeze
whether erik_halvorsen will exercise override authority is unknown
which offer kite_robotics will choose (volume discount vs co-marketing) is unknown
assumptions: halvorsen_procurement_committee uses simple majority for decisions
committee_decision_record requires at least 2 votes for validity
production_freeze applies to all new supply contracts at halvorsen_motors
kite_robotics can only submit one proposal at a time

auto_repairs: institution 'halvorsen_procurement_committee' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No representation of fall production freeze date or its dependency on external supply chain constraints
No entity or event for 'fall production freeze' as a deadline that can be moved or cancelled
No mechanism for negotiation failure (e.g., counteroffer, rejection, stalemate) beyond binary committee vote
No time constraints on committee meeting scheduling relative to freeze date
No representation of external market pressure or competitor offers that could accelerate or derail the partnership
relabeled_progress_bars: supplier_onboarding_process states are linear but ignore parallel offers (volume discount vs co-marketing) that could be accepted independently
production_freeze_process has no trigger for cancellation or postponement, only scheduled→active→completed
committee_decision_record does not capture tie-breaking or abstention, only approve/reject counts
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes committee vote is the only path to signing, but CEO Erik Halvorsen can override or expedite — no event for that override is defined
No event for 'proposal withdrawn' or 'offer expired' before freeze, which could make signing impossible even if committee approves

The outcome predicate 'partnership_signed_before_freeze' checks existence of signing_date but does not compare it to freeze_date — it only checks if signing_date exists at all, which is semantically wrong
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Amara Diallo's affordances include 'select offer type' but no event for changing offer type after submission
Sofia Brandt's affordances include 'set agenda items' but no event for delaying or fast-tracking a specific proposal
Erik Halvorsen's override affordance has no corresponding event type or transition in the process definition
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: committee_decision_record is treated as public outcome but no distinction between recorded vote and actual behind-the-scenes pressure or side deals
partnership_signed event assumes public signing is the only relevant act, ignoring private handshake agreements or conditional pre-signing commitments
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No calendar or clock mechanism to enforce the fall production freeze deadline
No economic or legal constraints (e.g., antitrust review, export controls) that could block signing
No representation of production capacity or inventory levels that might make the freeze irrelevant or urgent
